outbound_official.notes: Official Hungarian MFA Consular Information page listing countries where Hungarian citizens (ordinary private passport holders, M) may travel without a prior consular visa. Entries marked with D/SZ only (diplomatic/service passport required) are excluded from the visa_free list as they do not cover ordinary passport holders. Some entries have pre-travel online registration requirements (eTA / digital arrival card) but no prior consular visa. The page explicitly states this list is informational; travelers should verify current conditions with the destination country embassy before travel. Page accessed June 2026 via direct HTTP fetch from konzinfo.mfa.gov.hu (TLS chain issue on client).

visa_free.name: Algeria
visa_free.notes: Diplomatic/service passport only (30/90 days); ordinary passport NOT visa-free

visa_free.name: Belarus
visa_free.notes: Ordinary passport: 30 days (special conditions apply - see travel advice); diplomatic/service: 90 days

visa_free.name: China
visa_free.notes: Ordinary passport: 30 days (business, tourism, family visit, transit, networking only); diplomatic: 90 days; service: 30 days

visa_free.name: Croatia
visa_free.notes: 90 days; EU free movement; all current SZIG types accepted

visa_free.name: Kyrgyzstan
visa_free.notes: Ordinary passport: 30 days (from 1 January 2026; re-entry allowed after 30-day gap); diplomatic/service: 30 days

visa_free.name: Mexico
visa_free.notes: 180 days; tourism and business only for ordinary passport

visa_free.name: Mongolia
visa_free.notes: Ordinary passport: 30 days (verify with Mongolian embassy before travel); diplomatic/service: 90 days

visa_free.name: Montenegro
visa_free.notes: Ordinary passport + D + SZ: 90 days; SZIG: 30 days

visa_free.name: Peru
visa_free.notes: 90 days (immigration officer may grant fewer days; supporting documents recommended)

visa_free.name: Turkey
visa_free.notes: 90 days; SZIG card accepted for tourism/transit only

visa_free.name: Zambia
visa_free.notes: 90 days (tourism/private visit within 1 year); stays over 30 days require extension at immigration

visa_on_arrival.name: Oman
visa_on_arrival.notes: Ordinary passport: max 14 days tourism, visa issued on arrival; diplomatic/service: 90 days visa-free. Conditions: tourism purpose, return ticket, accommodation, insurance, passport valid 6+ months after departure

eta.name: United States
eta.notes: ESTA (Electronic System for Travel Authorization) required before boarding; valid for tourism/business up to 90 days. Biometric passport required.

eta.name: Canada
eta.notes: eTA (Electronic Travel Authorization) required for air travel and transit; up to 6 months. Other purposes (work, study) require visa.

eta.name: Kenya
eta.notes: Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) mandatory before each entry via etakenya.go.ke; 90 days

eta.name: Taiwan
eta.notes: Online registration via Taiwan immigration authority (Online Immigration Arrival Card) required before arrival; 90 days

eta.name: Malaysia
eta.notes: Malaysia Digital Arrival Card (MDAC) online pre-registration required; 90 days. Not a visa - entry remains visa-free.

visa_free.notes: All EU and EEA member state nationals have freedom of movement rights; no visa required for any duration.

eta.nationality: All visa-free nationals listed in EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II (except EU/EEA/CH citizens)
eta.notes: ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) will be mandatory for all visa-exempt third-country nationals entering Schengen area including Hungary. Confirmed for Q4 2026 launch, with a 6-month transitional period (travellers meeting all other entry conditions will not be refused entry solely for lacking ETIAS during transition, extending into approximately April 2027). ETIAS is valid for 3 years or until passport expires. Fee applies. As of June 2026, ETIAS is not yet operational.

visa_policy.notes: Hungary is a Schengen Area member. Visa-free nationals may stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period. All other third-country nationals require a Schengen visa (C visa) for short stays or a national D visa for long stays. The official list reflects EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II as implemented by Hungary. ETIAS (electronic travel authorization) will be required for visa-free visitors once operational (planned Q4 2026, with transition period into April 2027). Certain nationalities with bilateral agreements may stay an additional 30 or 90 days in Hungary beyond the standard Schengen allowance, subject to conditions (cannot travel to other Schengen states, must have not used full 90-day Schengen allocation in prior 6 months, must depart from Hungary).

conditional_access.conditions: Per EU Regulation No 265/2010 of 25 March 2010: a third-country national holding a valid long-stay visa (Type D) issued by any Schengen Member State may travel to Hungary and other Schengen states for up to 90 days in any 180-day period, under the same conditions as a residence permit holder. Must hold a valid travel document and meet other entry conditions.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Type C)
visa_types.purpose: Permits short-term tourism, family visits, or business activity in Hungary and the Schengen Area for nationals not eligible for visa-free entry.
visa_types.notes: Hungary is a Schengen member. Type C visas are issued by Hungarian consulates abroad. Allows up to 90 days in any 180-day period across the entire Schengen Area. Visa-free nationals do not need this visa. Applied for at the Hungarian embassy/consulate in the applicant's country of residence.

visa_types.name: Schengen Short-Stay Visa (Type C) - Business
visa_types.purpose: Covers short-term business activities in Hungary such as meetings, negotiations, and conferences for nationals of countries not on the Schengen visa-free list.
visa_types.notes: Business is a permitted purpose under the Schengen short-stay visa. For longer-term employment or self-employment a national long-stay (Type D) visa is required. Applied for at the Hungarian embassy/consulate.

visa_types.name: Airport Transit Visa (ATV)
visa_types.purpose: Permits transit through the international zone of a Hungarian airport without entering the Schengen Area, required for certain nationalities.
visa_types.notes: Required only for nationals of countries listed on the EU airport transit visa list. Does not permit entry into Hungary or other Schengen states. Applied for at the Hungarian consulate.

visa_types.name: EU Blue Card (Kék Kártya)
visa_types.purpose: Residence permit for highly qualified third-country nationals employed in Hungary in a job requiring tertiary education or equivalent professional experience.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 4 years, renewable. Minimum salary threshold: HUF 1,001,048/month (2026). Healthcare roles: HUF 800,838/month (2026). Employment restricted to sponsoring employer during first year. Must be applied for via Enter Hungary platform or Hungarian consulate. Grants access to EU-wide long-term mobility after 18 months.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Intra-Corporate Transfer
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals employed by a multinational group to be temporarily posted to a Hungarian entity within the same group for employment or training.
visa_types.notes: Executive/specialist permits valid up to 3 years. Trainee permits valid up to 1 year. Requires proof of at least 3 months continuous employment at the sending company. Not available to private entrepreneurs, agency workers, full-time students, or researchers. Long-term mobility (for ICT permit holders from other EU states) must be applied for 20 days before posting starts.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Posted Work
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals holding an EU employment residence permit to work in Hungary on a temporary posting from their EU-based employer.
visa_types.notes: Valid for up to 2 years, aligned with posting duration. Requires a valid EU employment residence permit from another Member State. Cannot exceed the original EU permit's validity. Does not lead to permanent residence. Extendable via Enter Hungary platform only.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Seasonal Employment
visa_types.purpose: Covers third-country nationals employed in plant production, forest management, animal breeding, or fishery sectors in Hungary on a seasonal basis.
visa_types.notes: Initial permit up to 6 months; extendable for up to 6 months within a 12-month period. Expedited 15-day processing for applicants with prior lawful stay in Hungary within last 5 years. Workers may only work for the designated employer listed on the permit. Employment termination invalidates permit after 6 days notice.

visa_types.name: Hungarian Card (Magyar Kártya)
visa_types.purpose: Work and residence permit for third-country nationals employed by a Hungarian employer, covering general employment not requiring the EU Blue Card level of qualification.
visa_types.notes: Referenced in official OIF factsheets as an employment-based permit. Standard employment category; distinct from EU Blue Card. Applied for via Enter Hungary platform or Hungarian consulate.

visa_types.name: White Card - Residency for Digital Nomads
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals working remotely for a foreign employer or managing their own foreign company to reside in Hungary for up to 2 years.
visa_types.notes: Initial validity up to 1 year, extendable once for another year (maximum 2 years total). Requires minimum net monthly income of EUR 3,000 for at least 6 months prior to application. Applicant must not work for any Hungarian employer or hold shares in Hungarian companies. Family reunification not permitted (except children born during the permit's validity). Cannot transition to other permit types or obtain permanent residence. Must be applied for via Enter Hungary platform or at the NDGAP directorate.

visa_types.name: Guest Investor Visa
visa_types.purpose: Entry visa for third-country nationals committing to a qualifying investment in Hungary before applying for the Guest Investor Residence Permit.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 6 months; becomes void upon issuance of Guest Investor Residence Permit. Two qualifying investment routes: (1) EUR 250,000 minimum in Hungarian National Bank-registered real estate investment fund shares (5-year holding required); (2) EUR 1,000,000 donation to a public higher education institution. Applied for at a Hungarian consulate in the applicant's country of residence, nationality, or legal stay. Residence permit application must be filed within 30 days of first entry into Hungary.

visa_types.name: Guest Investor Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Long-term residence permit for third-country nationals who make a qualifying investment of national economic interest in Hungary.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 10 years, renewable for additional 10-year periods indefinitely. Two investment routes: EUR 250,000 in real estate fund shares (5-year hold), or EUR 1,000,000 educational/cultural donation to a public higher education institution. Work permitted without restrictions including employment, self-employment, and chief executive roles. Guest investors cannot obtain a National Residence Card (permanent residence). Not a citizenship pathway. Processing time: 21 days. Applied for via Enter Hungary platform or regional NDGAP directorate.

visa_types.name: Guest Self-Employment Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals to pursue self-employed activities or act as chief executive of a business organization registered in Hungary.
visa_types.notes: Initial permit up to 1 year, extendable up to 3 years total. Self-employed must demonstrate annual income exceeding 24 times the minimum wage (via tax authority certification). Business executives: organization must employ at least 5 Hungarian/EEA nationals full-time for 6 consecutive months, or demonstrate sufficient income. Family reunification restricted for the first year after issuance. Cannot obtain National Residence Card (permanent residence). Cannot hold Hungarian Card or EU Blue Card simultaneously.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Traineeship
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals who recently obtained a higher education degree or are currently studying to undertake a traineeship with a Hungarian host organization.
visa_types.notes: Valid for up to 6 months; not renewable. Applicant must have obtained a higher education degree within 2 years prior, or be currently pursuing relevant studies. Requires a traineeship contract with an approved Hungarian host organization, language proficiency documentation, proof of financial resources, and health insurance. Host organization must commit to covering expulsion costs if needed.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Seeking a Job or Starting a Business
visa_types.purpose: Grants third-country nationals who held a research or study permit additional time to find employment or set up a business in Hungary.
visa_types.notes: Valid for maximum 9 months; not extendable. Only available to those transitioning from a research or study residence permit. Must apply via Enter Hungary platform at least 15 days before current permit expires. Holders may subsequently apply for a Hungarian Card, EU Blue Card, or Guest Self-Employment Residence Permit. Must register with employment services or provide employer certification of job commencement within 3 months.

visa_types.name: Researcher Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country national researchers to reside in Hungary while conducting research under a hosting agreement with an accredited Hungarian research organization.
visa_types.notes: Valid 1–2 years depending on hosting agreement duration. Long-term mobility permit for researchers from other EU Member States valid up to 365 days. Host organizations can submit applications on behalf of researchers via Enter Hungary. Family members of researchers may apply for their own permits. Initial examination within 15 days; final decision within 60 days.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Family Reunification
visa_types.purpose: Allows family members of Hungarian citizens, residence permit holders, refugees, or qualifying EEA nationals to reside in Hungary.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 3 years (extendable); 4 years if sponsor holds EU Blue Card or Corporate Card; 5 years if sponsor is Hungarian citizen with permanent residence; 10 years if sponsor is guest investor. Eligible family members: spouses, minor children (including adopted/foster), and dependent minors with parental custody. Processing time: 21 days standard, 70 days when combined with employment. Cannot exceed the validity of the applicant's travel document or sponsor's permit.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Medical Treatment
visa_types.purpose: Permits third-country nationals to reside in Hungary to receive medical treatment at a Hungarian medical facility, or to accompany a family member receiving treatment.
visa_types.notes: Valid up to 2 years; potentially extended for an additional 2 years based on treatment duration. Requires formal statement from the host medical facility. Processing time: 21 days standard, 8 days for minor applicants. Must enter Hungary within 3 months of approval or permit becomes invalid. Cannot apply for other permit types while holding this permit; does not lead to permanent residence. Family members cannot use family reunification provisions.

visa_types.name: Residence Permit for Voluntary Activities
visa_types.purpose: Allows third-country nationals to reside in Hungary to perform public interest voluntary activities under contract with an approved host organization or participate in European Voluntary Service.
visa_types.notes: Valid for the duration of the hosting contract, capped at 2 years. Not available to those with refugee status, subsidiary protection, tolerated status, or EU residence permits. Family members cannot obtain permits through family reunification. Does not lead to permanent residence eligibility. Cannot apply for other permit types while holding or after holding this permit.

visa_types.name: National Residence Card
visa_types.purpose: Permanent residence card for third-country nationals who have legally and continuously resided in Hungary for at least 3 years, granting the right to stay indefinitely.
visa_types.notes: Card valid up to 10 years, renewable for additional 10-year periods. Eligibility: 3 years continuous lawful residence, OR spouse of Hungarian citizen (married 2+ years), OR previously held Hungarian citizenship, OR minor children of long-term residents/recognized refugees, OR dependent ancestors residing with sponsor for 1+ years. Not available to those holding temporary work or study permits or to guest investors. Processing time 70 days standard; 5 days for children born in Hungary to eligible parents. Applications submitted via Enter Hungary platform only.

visa_types.name: EU Long-Term Residence Card (EU Residence Card)
visa_types.purpose: EU-standard permanent residence status for third-country nationals who have resided legally in Hungary for at least 5 years, conferring rights comparable to EU citizens within Hungary.
visa_types.notes: Card valid up to 10 years, renewable. Requires 5 years continuous lawful residence, or 2 years in Hungary plus 5 years total holding EU Blue Card, research, or study permits across EU Member States. Not available to students, seasonal workers, those under diplomatic immunity, asylum applicants, refugees, or those with tolerated status. Requires Hungarian cultural knowledge exam certificate. Applications via Enter Hungary platform only. Administrative fee: confirmed applicable, amount not extracted.

visa_types.name: Permanent Residence Card (EEA Nationals)
visa_types.purpose: Grants EEA nationals the right to permanent residence in Hungary after 5 years of continuous legal residence.
visa_types.notes: Available to EEA nationals who have resided continuously and legally in Hungary for 5 years, with accelerated pathways for workers reaching retirement age (3+ years residence), those forced to stop work due to illness (2+ years residence), accident-related incapacity, or workers commuting to another EEA state. Administrative fee: HUF 1,500 (paid electronically). Processing time: 70 days. Card remains valid indefinitely when paired with a valid travel/ID document. Application requires both in-person and electronic submission.

cbi.notes: Hungary does not have a citizenship-by-investment program. The Guest Investor Program provides a residence permit only (valid up to 10 years, renewable for additional 10-year periods), not a pathway to citizenship. Guest investors are explicitly excluded from obtaining a national residence card (permanent residence). Hungary does allow dual citizenship under its citizenship law; applicants for naturalization do not need to renounce prior nationality.

programs.name: Guest Investor Residence Permit
programs.notes: Two investment options: (1) Acquisition of investment fund share of at least EUR 250,000 issued by a real estate fund registered by the Hungarian National Bank - fund must allocate at least 40% of net asset value to Hungarian residential real estate; investor must hold for minimum 5 years. (2) Financial donation of at least EUR 1,000,000 to a public-trust higher education institution for educational, scientific research or artistic activities. A direct residential property purchase pathway (EUR 500,000) was abolished as of 15 January 2025. Permit valid up to 10 years, renewable for additional 10-year periods (no longer limited to one renewal). Guest investors explicitly CANNOT obtain a national residence card (permanent residence). No path to citizenship via this program. Work permitted without restrictions. Processing time: 21 days standard.

programs.name: EU Blue Card (Hungary)
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals with tertiary education or 3+ years relevant professional experience (within past 7 years for ICT/executive roles) working in a position requiring high-level qualifications. Minimum salary: HUF 883,671/month (2025); HUF 1,001,048/month (2026). Healthcare roles: HUF 706,937/month (2025); HUF 800,838/month (2026).
programs.processing_time: 70 days standard; 30 days for existing EU Blue Card holders from other EU member states
programs.notes: Initial validity up to 4 years, renewable for up to 4 years at a time. Employment restrictions apply during first year. 2026 salary thresholds apply retroactively to 2025-initiated applications with 2026 decisions. Healthcare 2026 threshold updated to HUF 800,838.

programs.name: White Card – Residency for Digital Nomads
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals with verified employment relationship or company ownership outside Hungary, working remotely from Hungary using digital technology. Must not pursue any gainful activity in Hungary or hold shares in Hungarian companies. Minimum net monthly income: EUR 3,000 for at least 6 months prior to entry.
programs.notes: Initial validity: maximum 1 year. Extendable once for additional 1 year (total maximum 2 years). White Card holders CANNOT obtain a national residence card (permanent residence). Family reunification is not available for White Card holders (except for children born during the permit's validity). Must have resided in Hungary for at least 90 days within any 180-day period before extension application.

programs.name: Guest Self-Employment Residence Permit
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals pursuing self-employed activities or serving as chief executive of a business organization. Self-employed must demonstrate annual income exceeding 24 times the current minimum wage (via tax authority certification). Business executives: organization must employ at least 5 Hungarian/EEA nationals full-time for 6 consecutive months, or demonstrate sufficient income.
programs.notes: Initial permit: maximum 1 year. Extendable up to 2 additional years (total maximum 3 years from first issuance). Holders CANNOT obtain a national residence card (permanent residence). Family reunification restricted for first year after issuance.

validation_notes: CORRECTION (2026-06-14): Blanket suspension of ALL bilateral diplomatic/service/official passport waivers until 6 March 2027 propagated to all 40 conditional_access passport_type entries. Prior record noted this suspension only for Georgia; official Hungarian consular source (konzuliszolgalat.kormany.hu) confirms it applies universally.

CORRECTION (2026-06-14): ETIAS timeline updated - confirmed Q4 2026 launch with 6-month transition period extending into approximately April 2027. Prior record stated 'planned Q4 2026' without noting the transition period.
CORRECTION (2026-06-14): EU Blue Card 2026 healthcare salary threshold added: HUF 800,838/month (was absent from prior record; confirmed from oif.gov.hu/factsheets/eu-blue-card).

CORRECTION (2026-06-14): RBI Guest Investor program notes updated - permit renewals confirmed as renewable for additional 10-year periods (not limited to one renewal as previously implied), per oif.gov.hu factsheet updated March 3, 2026.

ADDITION (2026-06-14): Bilateral ordinary-passport extended stay details added to visa_free notes for 20 countries (Andorra, Argentina, Brazil, Canada, Chile, Costa Rica, Hong Kong SAR, Israel, Japan, Macao SAR, Malaysia, Mexico, New Zealand, Panama, Paraguay, San Marino (30 days), Singapore (30 days), South Korea, USA, Uruguay, Venezuela) - confirmed from oif.gov.hu/factsheets/information-notice-for-persons-entitled-to-a-visa-free-stay.
CORRECTION (2026-06-14): White Card family reunification note corrected - children born during the permit's validity are an exception to the no-family-reunification rule, per oif.gov.hu factsheet.

VERIFIED (2026-06-14): Ordinary-passport visa-free list confirmed accurate against oif.gov.hu/factsheets/visa-free-citizens (68 states + Hong Kong SAR + Macao SAR + British special categories + Taiwan + Kosovo).
VERIFIED (2026-06-14): CBI: no program confirmed. Guest Investor program provides residence only.
VERIFIED (2026-06-14): Real estate direct purchase pathway (EUR 500,000) confirmed abolished 15 January 2025.

ADDITION (2026-06-14): outbound_official field added - Hungarian MFA official page 'Vízummentes külföldre utazás' (https://konzinfo.mfa.gov.hu/vizummentes-kulfoldre-utazas) confirmed as the origin-side authoritative list for where Hungarian ordinary passport holders can travel without a prior consular visa. Page content retrieved via direct HTTP fetch. Full country table extracted and structured into visa_free, visa_on_arrival, and eta categories.

CORRECTION (2026-06-27): REMOVED Vanuatu from visa_policy.visa_free. EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II was amended (M6, effective 3 February 2025) to move Vanuatu to Annex I (visa required) due to concerns about Vanuatu's investor citizenship scheme (golden passport program). The suspension of Vanuatu's visa waiver began May 4 2022 and became a permanent Annex I placement as of February 3 2025. Confirmed from EUR-Lex (CELEX:02018R1806-20250203). This was an error in the previous record.
VERIFIED (2026-06-27): EU Blue Card salary thresholds re-confirmed from oif.gov.hu/factsheets/eu-blue-card - HUF 1,001,048/month general, HUF 800,838/month healthcare (2026). No changes required.
VERIFIED (2026-06-27): Guest Investor Residence Permit confirmed from oif.gov.hu - EUR 250,000 fund option and EUR 1,000,000 donation option both still active. No changes required.

VERIFIED (2026-06-27): ETIAS Q4 2026 launch confirmed. EES became fully operational April 2026. ETIAS portal not yet open as of June 2026; launch expected October-November 2026. Mandatory with 6-month grace period (approximately April 2027).
VERIFIED (2026-06-27): Bilateral diplomatic/service/official passport suspension until 6 March 2027 confirmed still in effect per konzuliszolgalat.kormany.hu.
VERIFIED (2026-06-27): Dominican Republic correctly absent from visa_free list - confirmed Annex I (visa required) per EUR-Lex consolidated regulation.

VERIFIED (2026-06-29): visa_policy.visa_free list re-confirmed against oif.gov.hu/factsheets/visa-free-citizens (last updated March 3, 2026). No additions or removals detected. All 60+ entries remain accurate.

VERIFIED (2026-06-29): Bilateral extended-stay agreements re-confirmed from oif.gov.hu bilateral-agreement-based-continued-stay page (last updated March 17, 2026). Same 21-country list (20 x 90-day extensions + Singapore 30 days + San Marino 30 days). No changes.
VERIFIED (2026-06-29): ETIAS Q4 2026 launch and 6-month transition period (~April 2027 mandatory) confirmed from multiple sources including travel-europe.europa.eu. Record is accurate.
VERIFIED (2026-06-29): Diplomatic/service/official passport blanket suspension until 6 March 2027 confirmed still in effect per search results referencing konzuliszolgalat.kormany.hu.

VERIFIED (2026-06-29): No new EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II amendments found for 2025-2026 beyond the Vanuatu removal already recorded. Armenia visa liberalisation action plan published November 2025 but no visa-free access granted yet.
gaps: Exact scope of which passport types are covered by the blanket diplomatic/service/official suspension (until 6 March 2027): the official search result states 'diplomatic, service and official passports' but some bilateral agreements in the record include 'special' passports - unclear whether 'special' passports are covered by the suspension.
Exact naturalization residence requirement years for standard (non-simplified) naturalization pathway could not be confirmed from official English-language sources.

Biometric passport conditions for some Annex II countries (e.g., Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Georgia, Moldova, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia, Ukraine) noted in some sources but not explicitly stated in the official oif.gov.hu factsheet retrieved.
ETIAS fee amount not confirmed from official source.
Taiwan passport condition (must contain identity card number) noted in secondary sources but not extracted directly from official page.
Macao SAR biometric/specific passport type condition not explicitly verified from official English text.
OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) card: no official Hungarian or Indian government source found granting OCI cardholders any special entry privileges to Hungary. Not recorded.
Bilateral agreement dates/names: the official MFA table lists diplomatic/service/official passport waivers but does not provide agreement names or signing dates.

Full list of countries covered by blanket diplomatic/service/official passport suspension not independently extracted from the official table (konzuliszolgalat.kormany.hu had SSL error); suspension confirmed via search results referencing this page.

Whether Georgia's ordinary-passport visa-free status (Annex II) is affected by the diplomatic/service/official passport suspension - it is not; ordinary passport visa-free access for Georgia is under EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II and is unaffected.

outbound_official: Page date/last-updated timestamp not visible in fetched HTML (no 'asof' value captured). Belarus ordinary passport conditions require checking travel advice section for special conditions not detailed in the table. Oman ordinary passport described as visa-on-arrival (max 14 days) - this may change; travelers should verify.

outbound_official: Algeria, Bahrain, Cambodia, Cuba, Egypt, Ghana, India, Indonesia, Iran, Jordan, Kuwait, Laos, Lebanon, Maldives, Pakistan, Qatar, Russia, Turkmenistan, Yemen - these appear in the official outbound table only for diplomatic/service passport holders (not ordinary M passport); they are NOT visa-free for ordinary Hungarian passport holders and are excluded from outbound_official.visa_free.
